articles.title: Iran’s Afghan Refugees Flee Homeward
articles.excerpt: Thousands are returning daily at the Islam Qala border crossing as Afghans trade precarious life in Iran for an uncertain future in Afghanistan.

articles.title: Bringing Color Back to Syria
articles.excerpt: A reflection on wartime trauma among Syria’s children and the long road toward healing.

articles.title: The Devastation of Lebanon
articles.excerpt: Why many Lebanese view the latest strikes not as a war on Hezbollah, but as a war on everyone.

articles.title: Gaza in a Million Pieces
articles.excerpt: After six months, Damon is allowed into Gaza and finds a war being waged mainly against children.

articles.title: Former CNN reporter Arwa Damon on the war in Ukraine one year later and the need for aid in Turkey and Syria
articles.excerpt: Damon discusses the war in Ukraine, disaster response after the Turkey-Syria earthquake, and why she founded INARA.

articles.title: Gaza under siege: A doctor recounts the humanitarian cost of war
articles.excerpt: A firsthand account from surgeon Ghassan Abu Sitta of Gaza’s collapsing healthcare system under siege.

articles.title: I covered the battle against ISIS in Mosul. Gaza’s challenges will make it look like child’s play.
articles.excerpt: Damon draws hard-earned lessons from Mosul to explain why Gaza’s battlefield realities and humanitarian stakes are even more severe.

articles.title: I spent two decades as a CNN correspondent. We journalists need to do a better job reporting the truth.
articles.excerpt: An argument for journalism that confronts propaganda, dehumanization, and polarization—especially in wartime.

articles.title: Journalist Arwa Damon on Conflict, Humanity and the Stories That Stay With You
articles.excerpt: Arwa Damon reflects on two decades of war reporting, the psychological cost of bearing witness, and why she ultimately chose humanitarian work over the camera.

articles.title: On the Ground in Gaza — With Arwa Damon
articles.excerpt: Damon discusses her four entries into the Gaza Strip to lead INARA's humanitarian response, and the scenes she encountered that even decades of war reporting could not prepare her for.

articles.title: With Israel's Expanding War, My Colleagues in Gaza Fear a 'Death Blow'
articles.excerpt: A first-person account of the deteriorating humanitarian conditions in Gaza and the threat posed by plans to restructure aid delivery.

articles.title: Arctic Melt: Threat Beneath the Ice
articles.excerpt: Reporting from a Greenpeace expedition to the Arctic, Damon documents the alarming pace of polar ice loss and its consequences for global ecosystems.

articles.title: Palestinians Feel They Are Being 'Slowly Exterminated'
articles.excerpt: Damon speaks directly about the healthcare crisis inside Gaza and the moral weight of witnessing systematic destruction of civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: The arduous system for getting aid into Gaza
articles.excerpt: Arwa Damon’s charity — International Network for Aid, Relief and Assistance — has tried to send aid to Gaza, facing rejections and obstacles. The episode discusses the economics of running a humanitarian nonprofit and barriers to aid delivery.

articles.title: How generational trauma among Israelis and Palestinians fuels the cycles of violence
articles.excerpt: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Arwa Damon, former international foreign correspondent for CNN, about the impact of trauma on Israelis and Palestinians as a result of successive eruptions of violence.

articles.title: A 'Devastating Scale' Of Suffering In Syria
articles.excerpt: NPR's Lulu Garcia-Navarro speaks to CNN's Arwa Damon about the unfolding humanitarian crisis in Idlib as Turkish and Russian-backed Syrian government forces fight for control of the territory.

articles.title: For Days, Turkey Has Been Targeting Kurds In Northern Syria
articles.excerpt: As Turkey continues its attack against Kurdish towns in northern Syria, NPR's Lulu Garcia-Navarro speaks to CNN correspondent Arwa Damon, who is reporting near Turkey's border with Syria.

articles.title: CNN Defends Reporting On Slain Ambassador's Diary
articles.excerpt: CNN reporter Arwa Damon found Ambassador Stevens' personal journal in Benghazi, revealing his fears about security threats. The report sparked debate about journalistic ethics.

articles.title: A Passion To Bear Witness: Why War Correspondents Take The Risk
articles.excerpt: CNN's Arwa Damon made it into Homs, Syria, under siege, to report on the conflict. The article explores the motivations and risks for war correspondents.

articles.title: Trial Looms For Sole Defendant In 2012 Benghazi Attack That Killed Ambassador
articles.excerpt: Coverage of the Benghazi attack, with reporting from Arwa Damon on the ground, as the trial for the accused begins in Washington, D.C.

articles.title: INARA’s Mobile Clinic in Syria
articles.excerpt: INARA’s mobile clinic, led by Arwa Damon’s organization, reaches vulnerable communities in Syria, providing medical care in remote areas.

articles.title: A Humanitarian crisis on the Afghan-Iranian borders
articles.excerpt: INARA, founded by Arwa Damon, assesses urgent needs at the Afghan-Iranian border amid a rapidly escalating humanitarian crisis.

articles.title: Food parcels for displaced Gazan families in Egypt
articles.excerpt: INARA distributes food parcels to Gazan families displaced to Egypt, addressing food insecurity and financial hardship.

articles.title: As Gaza Braces for a Ground Invasion
articles.excerpt: Written just after Oct. 7 on the cycle of violence and emotional trajectories of both populations.

articles.title: One Year After Oct. 7
articles.excerpt: Co-authored piece examining how the region changed politically, socially, and militarily.

articles.title: Inside the Israeli Government's Starvation of Gaza
articles.excerpt: On INARA's attempts to deliver food amid the blockade.

articles.title: Syria + Trump Oval Office Meeting
articles.excerpt: Co-authored analysis on Trump hosting Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa.

videos.title: Arwa Damon on Her Motivation to Cover the Syrian Conflict
videos.description: Damon speaks candidly about what draws her to Syria, the human stories behind the statistics, and what motivates a journalist to keep returning to a warzone.

videos.title: Arwa Damon Investigates: Ivory War
videos.description: Embedded with anti-poaching rangers in the Republic of Congo's Odzala National Park, Damon investigates the illegal ivory trade decimating elephant populations.

videos.title: Children Are Crying Out in Agony for Food — Gaza Crisis
videos.description: Arwa Damon joins CNN's Wolf Blitzer to describe the catastrophic food shortage inside Gaza and INARA's efforts to deliver emergency nutrition.

videos.title: Arwa Damon on Her Motivation to Cover the Syrian Conflict
videos.description: Damon speaks candidly about what draws her to Syria, the human stories behind the statistics, and what motivates a journalist to keep returning to a warzone.

videos.title: Arwa Damon Investigates: Ivory War
videos.description: Embedded with anti-poaching rangers in the Republic of Congo's Odzala National Park, Damon investigates the illegal ivory trade decimating elephant populations.

videos.title: Children Are Crying Out in Agony for Food — Gaza Crisis
videos.description: Arwa Damon joins CNN's Wolf Blitzer to describe the catastrophic food shortage inside Gaza and INARA's efforts to deliver emergency nutrition.

interviews.title: She saw the death of the human soul
interviews.description: Long profile — on career from CNN to Gaza aid work.

interviews.title: Gaza's wounds and the humanitarian situation
interviews.description: Spoke with Jon Alterman about Gaza's wounds and the humanitarian situation.

interviews.title: Seizing the Summit with Journalist and Filmmaker Arwa Damon
interviews.description: Interview on Seizing the Summit documentary and INARA's work.

interviews.title: “The War Hasn’t Ended”: Palestinians in Gaza Still Face Israeli Attacks, Disease, Medical Neglect
interviews.description: Damon discusses ongoing conditions in Gaza, restrictions on medical care, and the continued humanitarian toll after a partial reopening of Rafah.

interviews.title: Inside Gaza: Arwa Damon on Crisis, Resilience, and Access
interviews.description: A discussion of humanitarian conditions in Gaza and the realities of delivering aid through INARA.

interviews.title: Inside Gaza: Arwa Damon on Crisis, Resilience, and Access
interviews.description: Damon shares a firsthand look at Gaza’s humanitarian crisis and what access constraints mean for civilians.

interviews.title: Is the Hunger Crisis in Gaza a Tipping Point in the War?
interviews.description: Damon joins CNN’s One Thing to discuss aid access, famine risk, and what would be required for meaningful relief in Gaza.

interviews.title: Battling Back Against Trump (Amanpour)
interviews.description: Damon appears on Amanpour to discuss humanitarian realities in Gaza and INARA’s work.

interviews.title: The War We Don’t See — with Arwa Damon
interviews.description: Damon discusses why wars fade from public attention, the personal cost of witnessing conflict, and being denied entry into Gaza.

interviews.title: The Emotional Fog of War — with Arwa Damon
interviews.description: A conversation about trauma, misinformation, and how emotion shapes decisions in conflicts like the Gaza war.

interviews.title: ‘Seize The Summit’: War refugees climb Kilimanjaro in a new documentary
interviews.description: Damon discusses her documentary 'Seize the Summit' following young war survivors climbing Mount Kilimanjaro.

interviews.title: “A Sea of Misery”: Gaza Is Unlike Anything I’ve Ever Seen
interviews.description: Damon describes conditions in Gaza after a humanitarian trip and argues for scaling up aid and humanitarian access.

interviews.title: “Beyond Atrocious”: Arwa Damon on Desperation & Hunger in Gaza as Israel Continues Blocking Most Aid
interviews.description: Damon discusses starvation conditions, barriers to aid delivery, and what she is witnessing through INARA’s work.

interviews.title: Lessons in compassion from the frontline
interviews.description: A TEDx talk on compassion, witnessing suffering, and why small acts of kindness matter in the aftermath of war.

interviews.title: As a CNN War Reporter, Arwa Damon Thought She Had Seen Everything. Then She Went to Gaza.
interviews.description: In a rare long-form interview, Damon opens up about leaving CNN, founding INARA, and how Gaza tested every limit of what she thought she knew about human suffering.

interviews.title: Media People: CNN's Arwa Damon on War Reporting, Tattoos and Close Calls
interviews.description: An intimate profile exploring Damon's personal philosophy, the physical and emotional toll of conflict journalism, and the stories she will never forget.

interviews.title: A Humanitarian Aid Crisis in Gaza — The Excerpt Podcast
interviews.description: Damon joins the podcast to break down the layers of the Gaza humanitarian crisis and what INARA's on-the-ground teams are witnessing.

interviews.title: Getting There: CNN's Arwa Damon
interviews.description: How a young woman who sold bathrobes online became one of the most recognisable faces of frontline war reporting. Damon traces her unlikely path to journalism.

interviews.title: The arduous system for getting aid into Gaza
interviews.outlet: NPR - The Indicator from Planet Money
interviews.description: Arwa Damon discusses the challenges of delivering humanitarian aid to Gaza and the obstacles faced by her nonprofit, INARA.

interviews.title: How generational trauma among Israelis and Palestinians fuels the cycles of violence
interviews.description: Arwa Damon, former CNN correspondent, discusses the psychological impact of conflict in the Middle East.

interviews.title: A 'Devastating Scale' Of Suffering In Syria
interviews.description: Arwa Damon reports on the humanitarian crisis in Idlib, Syria.

interviews.title: For Days, Turkey Has Been Targeting Kurds In Northern Syria
interviews.description: Arwa Damon provides on-the-ground reporting from the Turkey-Syria border.

interviews.title: A Passion To Bear Witness: Why War Correspondents Take The Risk
interviews.description: Arwa Damon shares her experiences as a war correspondent in Syria.
